findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\] def ofFinset" code/TauCeti/AlgebraicGeometry/WeilDivisor/FiniteSum.lean` reports lines 175 and 408; the named API is present at lines 317, 357, 364, 370, 377, 542, 559, 565, 571, and 577.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from both constructors and prove downstream facts through the named characteristic lemmas instead.
findings.issue: `ofFinsetWithMultiplicity` and `ofFinset` are marked `@[expose]` even though the file provides coefficient, support, degree, weighted-degree, and pushforward characterization lemmas, so consumers are encouraged to unfold to the `Finsupp.indicator` body.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "indicator_nat|indicator_one|ofFinset.*_eq_indicator" code/TauCeti/AlgebraicGeometry/WeilDivisor/FiniteSum.lean` finds public raw lemmas at lines 180-300 and 413-501 plus `ofFinsetWithMultiplicity_eq_indicator` at line 310 and `ofFinset_eq_indicator` at line 522.
findings.fix: Make the raw indicator lemmas private/internal and remove the public simp body restatements; keep `ofFinsetWithMultiplicity_*` and `ofFinset_*` as the public normal forms.
findings.issue: `@[simp]` lemmas rewrite the named constructors to raw `Finsupp.indicator` terms, and the file publicly exports parallel `indicator_nat`/`indicator_one` APIs for the same facts, making the implementation detail part of the public surface.

summary: The named constructors now exist, but the public API still exposes their `Finsupp.indicator` implementation and makes that raw term the simp normal form.
